creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
Michael,

thanks forwarded via IRC;
the thing is that easy-rsa is meant to operate on a local copy of "everything", and to that end, see the shiny new pkg-message -- copy from 

  https://cgit.freebsd.org/ports/tree/security/easy-rsa/pkg-message

which basically suggests: easyrsa --pki-dir=~/my_new_pki init-pki
Where the --pki-dir specifies where you want the new structure.

However it seems I need to rethink our FreeBSD-local wrapper which stomps all over EASYRSA if unset, and the script is not really prepared for that.

I will look into that, and then let's do a _2.
